reasoning: Analyzing this STEMI presentation. The patient is a 62-year-old male with ST elevation in V1-V4, elevated troponin, and classic substernal chest pressure. I need to compute risk scores and check for drug interactions given his current medications.

parameters.case_text: 62yo male, ST elevation V1-V4, troponin 2.4, substernal chest pressure

scores.risk_interpretation: TIMI score of 5 indicates approximately 12% 30-day mortality risk. Emergent reperfusion is indicated.
scores.recommendation: Emergent reperfusion therapy (PCI preferred over fibrinolysis when available within 120 min)

scores.risk_interpretation: GRACE score 168 indicates high risk. In-hospital mortality >3%. Recommend invasive strategy within 24h.
scores.recommendation: Early invasive strategy (cardiac catheterization within 24h); continuous monitoring in CCU

result.summary: Multi-score assessment confirms high-risk STEMI. TIMI 5/14, GRACE 168 all support emergent PCI.

reasoning: Risk scores confirm high-risk presentation. TIMI score 5/14 and GRACE 168 both indicate high risk. Now I need to check drug interactions — the patient is on aspirin 81mg, lisinopril 20mg, and atorvastatin 40mg. I need to verify safety of adding dual antiplatelet therapy and heparin.

interactions.description: Aspirin doses >100mg may reduce ticagrelor effectiveness. Maintain aspirin at 81mg.
interactions.recommendation: Keep aspirin at 81mg daily — do NOT increase dose

interactions.description: Combined anticoagulant and antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor aPTT closely, watch for signs of bleeding

result.summary: No absolute contraindications. Two moderate interactions noted — manageable with dose awareness and monitoring.

reasoning: Drug interaction check is clear — no absolute contraindications. The moderate interactions are expected and manageable. I can now synthesize a final assessment with high confidence. The diagnosis is acute anterior STEMI requiring emergent PCI.

key_findings: ST elevation in leads V1-V4 (anterior distribution)
Substernal chest pressure radiating to jaw
recommended_actions: Activate cath lab for emergent PCI
Administer dual antiplatelet therapy (aspirin 81mg + ticagrelor 180mg load)
Start heparin drip per ACS protocol (target aPTT 50-70s)
Obtain cardiology consult — door-to-balloon time < 90 min

agreements.finding: Acute anterior STEMI diagnosis based on ECG + troponin + clinical presentation
Emergent PCI is the indicated reperfusion strategy

model_a.name: MedGemma 27B
model_a.position: Start metoprolol 25mg PO within 24h if hemodynamically stable

model_b.name: Risk Score Engine
model_b.position: Defer beta-blocker until post-PCI hemodynamic reassessment

disagreements.resolution: Defer initiation until post-catheterization — reassess hemodynamics and LV function first

consensus.integrated_assessment: High-confidence STEMI diagnosis with concordant risk stratification across models. Emergent PCI indicated with standard dual antiplatelet therapy. Beta-blocker timing to be determined post-catheterization.
recommended_next_steps: Proceed to cath lab within 90 minutes of presentation
Echocardiography within 24h to assess LV function
